title: Merton Credit Risk Structural Model
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Merton Credit Risk Structural Model: Merton model: recover firm asset value V and asset volatility sigma_V from equity market cap and equity volatility. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.merton.structural…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~80; debt_complexity_multiple_tranches, default_boundary_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: E = V*N(d1) - D*e^{-rT}*N(d2) (equity=call on assets); sigma_E = (V/E)*N(d1)*sigma_V
E.world_state_x: firm asset value V and asset volatility sigma_V
E.observation_y: equity market cap E and equity volatility sigma_E from options

W.regime: Existence of the recovered firm_value_vol_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 80); debt_complexity_multiple_tranches dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Market gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: CDS spread prediction within 50 bp for investment grade. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~80. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |debt_complexity_multiple_tranches|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_firms * N_iter_Newton) for portfolio per iteration
